rationale_summary: The saved 1-candidate ablation file does not retain the detailed agent log. From the verified before/after state, the council appears to have repaired kinetics locally by increasing tau sharply while leaving Q and d unchanged.

Flow rate stayed unchanged, which indicates the single-candidate ablation did not explore an alternative throughput regime. It repaired the original design locally instead of moving to a different geometry region.
This larger reactor volume is the direct consequence of increasing tau while leaving flow rate and tubing diameter unchanged.
Tubing ID stayed at 1.6 mm. The single-candidate ablation file shows no evidence of a geometry search, so the council remained in the original large-ID regime.

The 1-candidate result corrected material from PFA to FEP. Its ablation file does not preserve agent text, but the richer 12-candidate log for the same SNAr case explicitly approved FEP compatibility with DMF at 150 C.

The 1-candidate result corrected BPR down from 10.0 to 0.6 bar. The detailed safety explanation was not retained in the ablation file, but the richer 12-candidate log for the same case estimated only about 0.62 bar was required.
Temperature stayed at 150 C. The saved 1-candidate output shows no chemistry-level change, only local repair of the operating point.
Concentration stayed at 0.15 M. The preserved proposal-level reasoning treats 0.15 M as the practical reactor concentration for the mixed feeds, and the ablation kept that value.
Reactor type remained a coil. The 1-candidate ablation did not change the process architecture.
Mixer type remained a T-mixer. The single-candidate result kept the same front-end mixing architecture.
Pressure drop increased only because the residence time was lengthened on the same geometry. This is a derived consequence, not a directly chosen field.

Surface-to-volume ratio did not improve because the tubing diameter never changed. This is the clearest sign that the 1-candidate run repaired the original design without finding a better geometry regime.
